body:
## Summary
- Convert `src/types/` and `src/lib/` from JS+JSDoc to full TypeScript (`strict` mode)
- Runtime: `tsx` (no compile step needed)
- `tsc --noEmit`: zero errors
- All 134 unit tests passing

## Changes
- **types**: 8 files — Card, Element, Rank, HandType, GameState/BattleState/RoundState, BossWeights/BossIntent, Buff (7 union types), Upgrade, Events
- **lib**: 3 files — deck (DeckState interface), hand (HandResult/ScoreResult), skills (ShieldState)
- **config**: `tsconfig.json` (target ES2024, module Node16, strict), `eslint.config.js` (+typescript-eslint), `Dockerfile` (tsx), `package.json` scripts

## Deps added
`typescript@6.0.3`, `tsx@4.21`, `@types/node/express/cors/jsonwebtoken/bcryptjs`, `@typescript-eslint/parser`, `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`

## Test plan
- [x] `tsc --noEmit` — zero type errors
- [x] `node --import tsx --test tests/unit/*.test.ts` — 134/134 passing
- [x] Old `.js` files in `pve/`, `routes/`, `models/` untouched — `allowJs` keeps them working

labels.name: typescript
labels.description: TypeScript migration or type system changes

reviews.body: Wow! A nice way to maintain a big codebase. Maybe I can use it in all backend codebase.

title: refactor(backend): migrate types and lib to TypeScript
